name: docs/guide/index.html — Self-Contained HTML User Guide + NIZAM.json docs Indexing

files_create.purpose: Author a single, fully self-contained HTML file to the full design contract of .agent/product_spec_003.md Sec 4 (§4.2's ten required content sections, in order): (1) Hero — one-sentence 'what it is' statement plus two persona CTAs ('Humans start here' / 'Agents load tools/SKILL.md via NIZAM.json'). (2) Problem → Solution — four cards, one per DD-1..DD-4, each naming the flaw it remediates and the mechanism (source: .agent/product_spec.md Sec 4 and README.md Design Decisions — DD-1 Tool-Driven State Management/NIZAM.json query-not-bulk-read, DD-2 Universal Circuit Breaker/3-strike limit, DD-3 Evidence Externalisation/.agent/evidence/, DD-4 Unified Skill Payload/single tools/ payload). (3) Quickstart — the pinned-tag bootstrap.sh one-liner using a real git tag syntax (GOVERNANCE_TAG=vX.Y.Z or --tag vX.Y.Z, sourced from bootstrap.sh's usage text and standard/GIP.md Section 2 point 1's floating-branch refusal), the resulting .nizam/ tree (standard/, templates/, schema/, tools/, NIZAM.json — bootstrap.sh REQUIRED_MODULE_DIRS plus NIZAM.json), and the --verify-only network-free drift check (bootstrap.sh's run_verify_only). (4) Adoption paths — a new-repo walkthrough (Section 2's pinned-tag-clone-inject-verify sequence) AND the existing-repo incremental tiers from standard/GIP.md Section 5 (5.1 conflict rules for pre-existing CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI — rename-and-diff, never silent overwrite; 5.2's three tiers: docs-standard-only, +templates, full loop). (5) The execution loop — a visual diagram (semantic HTML/CSS, no image asset) of planner → contract (status: proposed) → Validator Mode A → Evaluator contract review → implement → Validator Mode B → Evaluator QA verdict, annotated with the 3-strike circuit breaker (source: standard/AGF.md Sec 3-4, methodology/01_execution.md Sec 2-5, methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md Sec 3-4, tools/SKILL.md Sec 3-4). Must literally name 'circuit breaker' and both 'Mode A' and 'Mode B'. (6) Anatomy of a governed repo — an annotated file tree including the .agent/ artifact families and their lifecycle: run_state.json, feature_list.json, contracts/NNN.json, qa/NNN.json, evidence/*.txt (source: methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 4, tools/skill.json state_interface.artifact_families). (7) Module reference cards — one card per module (standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, tools/), each naming its purpose and linking its real key_documents repo-relative paths (source: NIZAM.json modules array — every path named must exist on disk). (8) Agent integration — tools/skill.json discovery in the POST-H4 corrected order (tools/interface.md Section 2: .nizam/tools/skill.json FIRST for a bootstrapped consumer, then the repository-root tools/skill.json 'framework checkout' fallback, then pinned-checkout discovery), the three abstract operations (read-state, write-evidence, run-verification — tools/interface.md Sec 4), and a conformance-checklist digest (tools/interface.md Sec 5, condensed, not verbatim-copied in full). (9) FAQ — covering what Nizam is NOT (pure governance/methodology payload; ships no application code, infrastructure, or runtime services — .agent/product_spec.md Sec 1), versioning/pinning (semantic-version git tags, never a floating branch — standard/GIP.md Sec 2 point 1), drift detection (standard/GIP.md Sec 4 — re-bootstrap, never hand-patch), and license (a one-line pointer noting the LICENSE decision is a separate human-gated feature, F-021, without asserting a specific license choice since F-021 has not landed as of this contract). (10) Footer — the framework version equal to NIZAM.json framework.version (see the Version Consistency Anchor below) and canonical links using only github.com/niq-cnr hostnames. Structural/technical requirements (Sec 4, Sec 4.3): inline <style> and inline <script> only — zero <link rel=stylesheet> and zero <script src=...>; zero external network requests except href/src URLs under https://github.com/ or https://niq-cnr.github.io/; a system font stack (no @font-face / webfont loads); a prefers-color-scheme media query providing both dark and light rendering; semantic HTML5 elements (header/nav/main/section/footer); WCAG-AA contrast in both color schemes; print-friendly (a @media print block is acceptable but not separately acceptance-tested); a sticky in-page <nav> table of contents using position: sticky. Any inline <script> may only implement in-page behavior (e.g. TOC active-section highlighting, dark/light toggle override) — no network calls, no external library references. Version Consistency Anchor (Sec 4.1): read NIZAM.json's framework.version field (its actual current value, 0.1.0 — do NOT hardcode a different or guessed value, and do NOT bump NIZAM.json itself, which is this contract's non-goal) and render that EXACT string in BOTH a <meta name="framework-version" content="<version>"> element in <head> AND inside the page's <footer> element (Sec 4.2 item 10). Acronym expansion (Sec 4.2 closing paragraph): NDS (Nizam Documentation Standard), AGF (Agent Governance Framework), GIP (Governance Inheritance Protocol), and AGIP (the predecessor prototype bootstrap.sh evolved from, per bootstrap.sh's own header comment) are each expanded in full at their first use in the guide's body text. External Anchor Rule (Sec 4, Sec 1): every factual claim traces to the shipped tree named above — bootstrap.sh, tools/interface.md (post-F-019 corrected discovery order), standard/GIP.md (post-F-019 4-dir+NIZAM.json payload, drift detection, Sec 5 existing-repo tiers), standard/AGF.md, methodology/01_execution.md, methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md, tools/SKILL.md, .agent/product_spec.md Sec 4 and README.md (DD-1..DD-4), methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md and tools/skill.json (.agent/ artifact families), NIZAM.json modules (module reference cards), and docs/architecture/ADR-002-html-user-guide.md (the guide's own decision record). The guide invents no behavior beyond what these documents already state. Every repository-relative path named in the guide (any substring matching standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, tools/, or docs/ followed by a path segment) MUST resolve to a real file on disk. No 'nizamiq', '.svc', or 'cluster.local' string may appear anywhere in the file.

files_modify.purpose: Index the now-real docs/ documentation this feature creates. Add exactly one new entry to the top-level modules array: {"path": "docs", "purpose": <one-line summary of shipped framework documentation — ADRs and the HTML user guide>, "key_documents": ["docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md", "docs/architecture/ADR-002-html-user-guide.md", "docs/guide/index.html"]}. This satisfies validator C4 (registry/nizam-index.schema.json's modules[].path/purpose/key_documents required shape, each key_documents entry resolving on disk) without requiring any capabilities[] array change (module-level indexing is sufficient per the feature description's 'a docs module and/or capability entries' framing). Because the new module's path ('docs') is not one of bootstrap.sh's REQUIRED_MODULE_DIRS (standard/templates/schema/tools), bootstrap.sh's check_nizam_index function correctly continues to ignore it — docs/ is shipped documentation, not part of the consumer-injected payload (product_spec.md Sec 2.1, ADR-002). Do NOT change framework.version (the release version bump is F-022's human gate, per product_spec_003.md Sec 4.1 and Sec 5's F-022 description). Do NOT modify any other top-level key (capabilities, schemas, templates, consumption_guidance, index_schema, self_reference) or any existing modules[] entry. The file MUST remain valid JSON and continue to validate against registry/nizam-index.schema.json after the edit.

non_goals: No CONTEXT.md or README.md edit of any kind — the first-contact truth fixes and the guide link-in belong to feature 020
No CHANGELOG.md edit of any kind — phase-close CHANGELOG entries for phase-003 deliverables belong to feature 022

No edit to any standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, or tools/ document — those modules' content is out of this feature's remediation scope (H4/H5/H6/H8 corrections belong to the already-complete feature 019)
No edit to .agent/product_spec_003.md or .agent/product_spec.md — both are this contract's spec inputs (or a prior feature's completed deliverable), not deliverables of this contract
No edit to any file beyond docs/guide/index.html, NIZAM.json, and this contract file itself
No change to NIZAM.json framework.version — the release version bump is feature 022's human gate
No change to any existing NIZAM.json modules[] entry, or to the capabilities/schemas/templates/consumption_guidance/index_schema/self_reference top-level keys
No JavaScript network call, external library reference, external font load, or external stylesheet/script tag in docs/guide/index.html — the guide is fully self-contained per the design contract

No invented behavior in the guide — every factual claim must trace to the shipped tree named in this contract's scope.files_create purpose (the External Anchor Rule); the guide does not describe aspirational or future behavior as if already shipped

verification.command: test -f docs/guide/index.html && echo PASS || { echo "MISSING docs/guide/index.html"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Guide is valid HTML (html.parser feeds it without raising): python3 -c 'from html.parser import HTMLParser; HTMLParser().feed(open("docs/guide/index.html", encoding="utf-8").read())'
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import sys
from html.parser import HTMLParser
path = sys.argv[1]
HTMLParser().feed(open(path, encoding='utf-8').read())
print('PASS')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Guide is fully self-contained — no external stylesheet or script: ! grep -Eq '<link[^>]+rel=["'\''"]?stylesheet|<script[^>]+src=' docs/guide/index.html
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import re, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
if re.search(r'<link[^>]+rel=["\']?stylesheet', text, re.IGNORECASE) or re.search(r'<script[^>]+src=', text, re.IGNORECASE):
    print('external stylesheet or script src found')
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Guide makes zero external requests other than github.com / niq-cnr links: quote-agnostic (double/single/unquoted) href|src scan of https:// URLs, asserting each matches github.com or niq-cnr.github.io
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import re, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
urls = re.findall(r'(?:href|src)\s*=\s*[\x22\x27]?(https?://[^\x22\x27\s>]+)', text)
bad = [u for u in urls if not re.match(r'https?://(github\.com|niq-cnr\.github\.io)/', u)]
if bad:
    print('bad external url(s): ' + ', '.join(bad))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'prefers-color-scheme' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing prefers-color-scheme"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Guide has a sticky in-page TOC: a CSS rule whose selector names a nav-related element (or an inline style on a <nav> element) actually sets position: sticky -- not merely independent presence of '<nav' and 'position: sticky' anywhere in the file
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import re, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
style_match = re.search(r'<style[^>]*>(.*?)</style>', text, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
style_content = style_match.group(1) if style_match else ''
found = False
for sel, decl in re.findall(r'([^{}]+)\{([^}]*)\}', style_content, re.DOTALL):
    if 'nav' in sel.lower() and re.search(r'position\s*:\s*sticky', decl, re.IGNORECASE):
        found = True
        break
if not found:
    for m in re.finditer(r'<nav\b[^>]*style=\x22([^\x22]*)\x22', text, re.IGNORECASE):
        if re.search(r'position\s*:\s*sticky', m.group(1), re.IGNORECASE):
            found = True
            break
if not found:
    print('no CSS rule or inline style targeting a nav-related selector sets position: sticky (co-occurrence not found)')
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Guide embedded framework-version equals NIZAM.json framework.version, checked in BOTH the <meta name="framework-version"> content attribute AND the <footer> element's rendered text (not presence alone)
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import json, re, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
version = json.load(open('NIZAM.json', encoding='utf-8'))['framework']['version']
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
m = re.search(r'<meta\s+name="framework-version"\s+content="([^"]*)"', text)
if not m:
    print('no <meta name="framework-version"> element found')
    sys.exit(1)
if m.group(1) != version:
    print('meta content ' + repr(m.group(1)) + ' != NIZAM.json framework.version ' + repr(version))
    sys.exit(1)
fm = re.search(r'<footer\b.*?</footer>', text, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
if not fm:
    print('no <footer> element found')
    sys.exit(1)
if version not in fm.group(0):
    print('footer element missing exact version string ' + repr(version))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Every repository-relative path the guide names (standard|methodology|registry|templates|schema|tools|docs prefixed), after stripping trailing sentence/markup punctuation (period, comma, closing paren/brace-adjacent punctuation, backtick, quote), resolves on disk, and at least one such path is named
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import re, os, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
raw_paths = re.findall(r'(?:standard|methodology|registry|templates|schema|tools|docs)/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+', text)
paths = set()
for p in raw_paths:
    p = p.rstrip('.,);:\x60\x22\x27')
    if p:
        paths.add(p)
missing = [p for p in paths if not os.path.exists(p)]
if missing:
    print('missing path(s): ' + ', '.join(sorted(missing)))
    sys.exit(1)
if not paths:
    print('no repository-relative paths named in guide')
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS (' + str(len(paths)) + ' paths resolved)')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Guide contains no nizamiq branding (guide is outside validate.sh C5): ! grep -qi 'nizamiq' docs/guide/index.html
verification.command: f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; if grep -qi 'nizamiq' "$f"; then echo 'nizamiq branding string found'; grep -ni 'nizamiq' "$f"; exit 1; else echo PASS; fi

verification.acceptance_test: Guide renders all four design-decision cards: for dd in DD-1 DD-2 DD-3 DD-4; do grep -q "$dd" docs/guide/index.html || exit 1; done
verification.command: f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; fail=0; for dd in DD-1 DD-2 DD-3 DD-4; do grep -q "$dd" "$f" || { echo "missing $dd"; fail=1; }; done; test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: Guide covers the execution loop and circuit breaker: grep -qi 'circuit breaker' docs/guide/index.html AND grep -q 'Mode A' docs/guide/index.html AND grep -q 'Mode B' docs/guide/index.html (conjunction -- all three literal terms required, not an either/or alternation)
verification.command: f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -qi 'circuit breaker' "$f" || { echo "missing circuit breaker mention"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'Mode A' "$f" || { echo "missing literal Mode A mention"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'Mode B' "$f" || { echo "missing literal Mode B mention"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NIZAM.json indexes both ADRs and the guide: grep -q 'docs/architecture/ADR-001' NIZAM.json && grep -q 'docs/architecture/ADR-002' NIZAM.json && grep -q 'docs/guide/index.html' NIZAM.json
verification.command: f=NIZAM.json; grep -q 'docs/architecture/ADR-001' "$f" || { echo "NIZAM.json missing ADR-001 path"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'docs/architecture/ADR-002' "$f" || { echo "NIZAM.json missing ADR-002 path"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'docs/guide/index.html' "$f" || { echo "NIZAM.json missing docs/guide/index.html path"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NIZAM.json still validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json AND the new docs module's key_documents entries each resolve on disk (robustness beyond bare schema validation, which does not itself check the filesystem)
verification.command:
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
import jsonschema
data = json.load(open('NIZAM.json', encoding='utf-8'))
schema = json.load(open('registry/nizam-index.schema.json', encoding='utf-8'))
jsonschema.validate(data, schema)
docs_module = next((m for m in data.get('modules', []) if m.get('path') == 'docs'), None)
if docs_module is None:
    print('no docs module found in NIZAM.json modules[]')
    sys.exit(1)
missing = [d for d in docs_module.get('key_documents', []) if not os.path.isfile(d)]
if missing:
    print('docs module indexed path(s) failed to resolve: ' + ', '.join(missing))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF

verification.acceptance_test: Validator stays green on the clean tree (C4 index-integrity incl. the new docs paths): bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (robustness, revised from the contract-014/016/019 git-diff-based precedent, which is structurally blind to brand-new untracked files such as this feature's own docs/guide/index.html deliverable): git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all (excluding .agent/) reports, after stripping each line's 3-character status prefix, only paths in {docs/guide/index.html, NIZAM.json} -- no other new or modified file exists in the working tree
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- . ':(exclude).agent' | cut -c4-); bad=""; while IFS= read -r f; do [ -z "$f" ] && continue; case "$f" in docs/guide/index.html|NIZAM.json) ;; *) bad="$bad|$f" ;; esac; done <<< "$changed"; if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "out-of-scope new/modified file(s):$bad"; exit 1; fi; echo "PASS (status-scanned: $changed)"

verification.acceptance_test: Acronyms NDS, AGF, and GIP are each expanded to their full canonical phrase (sourced verbatim from standard/NDS.md, standard/AGF.md, and standard/GIP.md's own shipped titles) at least once in the guide; AGIP appears with an adjacent parenthetical expansion, mirroring the identical acceptance-test pattern feature_list_003.json already uses for bootstrap.sh (F-020) and CHANGELOG.md (F-022), since no canonical AGIP expansion phrase exists anywhere in the shipped tree to source verbatim (H7)
verification.command:
f=docs/guide/index.html; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 - "$f" <<'PYEOF'
import re, sys
path = sys.argv[1]
text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
missing = []
if 'Nizam Documentation Standard' not in text:
    missing.append('NDS -> \x22Nizam Documentation Standard\x22 (standard/NDS.md title)')
if 'Agent Governance Framework' not in text:
    missing.append('AGF -> \x22Agent Governance Framework\x22 (standard/AGF.md title)')
if 'Governance Inheritance Protocol' not in text:
    missing.append('GIP -> \x22Governance Inheritance Protocol\x22 (standard/GIP.md title)')
if not re.search(r'AGIP \(|\([^)]*AGIP\)', text):
    missing.append('AGIP -> no adjacent parenthetical expansion found (pattern: \x22AGIP (\x22 or \x22(...AGIP)\x22, matching the F-020/F-022 acceptance-test precedent, since no canonical AGIP expansion is shipped anywhere in the repo yet -- H7)')
if missing:
    print('missing acronym expansion(s): ' + '; '.join(missing))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
PYEOF

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/018-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/018-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/018-verify-16.txt for the sixteenth). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
